note:
Tatmadaw的一位发言人表示，有关军事行动和阵地的敏感信息泄露，是阿拉坎邦部分地区上周末进入第二个年头的互联网禁令的主要原因之一。
Tatmadaw True News新闻组秘书Zaw Min Tun准将于6月23日在Nay Pyi Taw举行的新闻发布会上发表讲话。
他解释说：“军事信息，比如哪个军事纵队正在从什么位置移动到哪个地区，这些信息都上传到了社交媒体上。”“而且有一些信息让国内外的人误解了鞑靼人。因此，出于安全原因，我们不得不关闭该地区的互联网。“。
佐敏敦称，互联网禁运也是为了阻止极端主义言论、仇恨言论和误导性信息的传播。他说，缅甸政府军目前还没有计划向政府建议解除禁令。
6月21日是七个阿拉坎州乡镇和钦邦帕莱特瓦镇实施互联网封锁一周年。
在过去的一年里，长期剥夺互联网接入的做法招致了很多批评，本周Pyithu Hluttaw议员U Pe Than又进行了抨击。
“在其他国家，互联网没有被关闭，以避免仇恨言论和(泄露)军事事务。如果(在其他国家)采取这样的行动，禁令很快就会解除。我们国家已经实施互联网禁令一年了。这不应该发生，“代表Myebon镇的U Pe Than说。
他建议，政府应该使用其他方法解决问题，并应该解除互联网禁令。
言论自由倡导组织Athan的研究经理Ko Ye Wai表示，民主政府有责任充分保护人民的言论自由权利。
他补充说，对于容易受到潜在人权侵犯的饱受战争蹂躏的人来说，互联网接入是一个重要的问责机制。
“有必要结束互联网禁令。来自受战争影响地区的人们是这场战争的受害者。如果他们没有互联网接入，他们可能面临更大的生命、财产和繁荣损失的风险。居民的主要希望是有互联网接入，“柯业伟说。
2019年6月21日，政府对阿拉坎州的Buthidaung、Maungdaw、Rathedaung、Ponnagyun、Kyauktaw、Myebon、Mrauk-U和Minbya镇以及Paletwa镇实施了互联网禁令。这项禁令于9月1日在一些乡镇解除，但在2月3日重新实施。孟都镇于5月2日第二次恢复互联网接入。
阿拉干和钦邦受影响的八个乡镇仍有100多万人没有互联网接入。
note_en:
A Tatmadaw spokesperson said the leaking of sensitive information about military operations and positions was one of the primary reasons for an internet ban in parts of Arakan State that entered its second year over the weekend.
 The secretary of the Tatmadaw True News Information Team, Brigadier-General Zaw Min Tun, was speaking at a news conference in Nay Pyi Taw on June 23.
 “Military information such as which military column is moving from what location to which area is uploaded on social media,” he explained. “And there is some information that makes people in the country and abroad misunderstand the Tatmadaw. So, we have to shut down the internet in the region for security reasons.”
 Zaw Min Tun described the internet embargo as also intended to put a stop to the dissemination of extremist rhetoric, hate speech and misleading information, saying the Tatmadaw had no plan as yet to recommend a lifting of the ban to the government.
 June 21 marked the one-year anniversary of the internet blackout, imposed in seven Arakan State townships and Chin State’s Paletwa Township.
 The long-running deprivation of internet access has drawn much criticism over the past year, and this week Pyithu Hluttaw lawmaker U Pe Than piled on.
  “In other countries, the internet is not shut down to avoid hate speech and [leaking of] military affairs. If such action is imposed [in other countries], the ban is lifted in short order. Our country has imposed the internet ban for a year. It should not happen,” said U Pe Than, who represents Myebon Township.
 The government should resolve its issues using other approaches and should lift the internet ban, he suggested.
 Ko Ye Wai, research manager of the free speech advocacy group Athan, said a democratic government is responsible for fully protecting people’s right to freedom of expression.
 He added that internet access was an important accountability mechanism for a war-ravaged people vulnerable to potential human rights abuses.
 “It is necessary to end the internet ban. People from war-affected areas are victims of the war. If they do not have internet access, they might face greater risk of losing their lives, property and prosperity. The main hope for the residents is to have internet access,” Ko Ye Wai said.
 The government imposed its internet ban on Buthidaung, Maungdaw, Rathedaung, Ponnagyun, Kyauktaw, Myebon, Mrauk-U and Minbya townships in Arakan State, and Paletwa Township, on June 21, 2019. The ban was lifted in some of the townships on September 1, only to be reimposed on February 3. Maungdaw Township saw its internet access restored for a second time  on May 2.
 More than 1 million people across the eight affected townships in Arakan and Chin states remain without internet access.
title: 军方坚定不移地捍卫缅甸西部的互联网封锁
title_en: Military Stands Firm In Defense Of Western Myanmar's Internet Blackout

特朗普政府呼吁Facebook、Twitter和其他科技巨头对呼吁人们违反宵禁、实施暴力行为和推倒与全国种族正义抗议活动有关的雕像的帖子采取行动，称这些内容是将美国人的安全置于危险境地的“犯罪活动”。
根据与“华盛顿邮报”共享的副本，国土安全部(Department Of Homeland Security)周五向科技业高管发出的信件中，代理部长查德·沃尔夫(Chad Wolf)写道，流行的社交媒体网站似乎在助长“入室盗窃、纵火、严重袭击、骚乱、抢劫和污损公共财产”方面发挥了作用。
据一名不愿透露姓名的高级官员透露，特朗普政府的律师也一直在研究如何利用自己的法律权力来回应他们认为非法或暴力的内容。这名高级官员要求匿名，以描述一次私人审议。此举出台之际，美国政府正在更广泛地考虑修改法律，这些法律多年来一直使在线网站和服务不必为用户发布的内容承担责任。
在乔治·弗洛伊德去世后，美国面临着来自暴力极端分子的前所未有的威胁，他们试图将他的死亡悲剧用于非法目的，“沃尔夫在致苹果、Snap和Alphabet等公司的信中写道，Alphabet是谷歌和YouTube的母公司。
他补充说：“在国土安全部，我们致力于保护美国人民、我们的家园和我们的价值观，包括保护我们的第一修正案的权利，同时保护我们的公民、执法人员和财产的安全。”
在信中，沃尔夫没有提到任何具体的社交媒体帖子或政治团体，而是选择了广泛的呼吁，要求硅谷帮助“结束”对人和财产的攻击，他说，这些攻击在一定程度上是由主要的在线平台促成的。他要求科技巨头采取更激进行动的要求，可能会在一些人中面临不确定的反应，这些人将社交媒体网站视为伤害的渠道，但也认为特朗普总统错误地将暴力行为归咎于抗议者。
弗洛伊德在明尼阿波利斯警方拘留期间被杀后，全国各地的大多数示威活动都是和平的，一些更暴力的爆发被归咎于执法官员或右倾民兵组织。
联邦官员已经对多名与右翼“恶作剧”意识形态有关的人提起诉讼，这种意识形态在Facebook和Twitter等网站上获得了势头。检察官指控内华达州的三名男子试图向美国林务局的一座建筑投掷燃烧弹，并指控加州的两名据称是恶鬼追随者杀害了一名联邦保安。
与此同时，特朗普和他的高级助手们继续辩称，“反法”组织，或称极左翼活动人士，对抢劫和其他暴力行为负有责任。特朗普试图将反法组织贴上恐怖组织的标签，尽管全国各地的地方执法官员对这些说法表示怀疑。
特朗普还严厉谴责了试图在全国范围内推翻南部邦联和其他雕像和纪念碑的抗议者，他一度在推特上发帖称，如果抗议者摧毁纪念碑，他希望将抗议者监禁长达10年。本周，美国法警也收到了保护这些建筑的通知，抗议者认为这些建筑是种族主义文物。本周，威斯康星州麦迪逊激烈的示威活动演变成暴力事件，导致两座雕像倒塌，一名州议员受伤。
note_en:
The Trump administration is calling on Facebook, Twitter and other tech giants to take action against posts that call for people to break curfews, commit violent acts and topple statues in connection with racial-justice protests nationwide, describing such content as “criminal activity” that puts Americans’ security at risk.
  The requests came in letters to top tech executives sent Friday by the Department of Homeland Security, whose acting secretary, Chad Wolf, wrote that popular social media sites appear to have played a role in facilitating “burglary, arson, aggravated assault, rioting, looting, and defacing public property,” according to copies shared with The Washington Post.
 Lawyers for the Trump administration also have been looking into ways they can use their legal authorities in response to content they see as illegal or violent, according to a senior official who spoke on the condition of anonymity to describe a private deliberation. The move comes as the U.S. government more broadly is looking at  overhauling laws that for years have spared online sites and services from being held liable for the content  posted by their users.
  "In the wake of George Floyd’s death, America faced an unprecedented threat from violent extremists seeking to co-opt the tragedy of his death for illicit purposes,” Wolf wrote to companies including Apple, Snap and Alphabet, the parent company of Google and YouTube.
   “At the Department of Homeland Security, we are committed to safeguarding the American people, our homeland, and our values, which includes protecting our First Amendment rights while keeping our citizens, law enforcement officers, and property safe,” he added.
   In his letters, Wolf did not reference any specific social media posts or political groups, opting instead for a broad call for Silicon Valley to help “put an end” to attacks on people and property that he said were facilitated in part by major online platforms. His requests for tech giants to take more-aggressive action could face uncertain reception among those who see social media sites as conduits for harm but also believe President Trump wrongly has blamed protesters for violent acts.
   Most demonstrations across the country in the aftermath of Floyd’s killing in Minneapolis police custody have been peaceful affairs, and some of the more violent outbursts have been attributed to law enforcement officials or  right-leaning militia groups.
 Federal officials have filed charges against multiple people tied to a the right-wing “boogaloo” ideology that has gained momentum on sites including Facebook and Twitter. Prosecutors allege three men  in Nevada sought to firebomb a U.S. Forest Service building and have charged two alleged boogaloo followers in California with killing a federal security guard.
  In the meantime, Trump and his top aides continue to argue that “antifa” groups, or far-left activists, are responsible for looting and other acts of violence. Trump has  sought to label antifa as a terrorist organization even as local law enforcement officials nationwide  have cast doubt on those claims.
   Trump also has sharply condemned protesters who have sought to topple Confederate and other statues and monuments nationwide, at one point tweeting he hoped to  imprison protesters for up to 10 years if they destroyed monuments. U.S. marshals this week  also were put on notice to protect these structures, which protesters see as racist relics. Heated demonstrations in Madison, Wis.,  turned violent this week, toppling two statues and injuring a state lawmaker.
title: 特朗普对Facebook，Twitter的管理：结束推翻雕像的呼声，做“暴力”行为
title_en: Trump admin to Facebook, Twitter: end calls to topple statues, do 'violent’ acts

苹果系统管理员在宣布收购消息的当天上午开始在社交媒体上抱怨，称公司不再允许他们连接第三方应用程序。
“主要是Fleetsmith维护了一个第三方应用程序目录，这样你就可以在你的Mac上部署Chrome或Zoom之类的东西，Fleetsmith会维护这些应用程序的安全更新。这是我们购买Fleetsmith的主要原因，“一位Fleetsmith客户告诉TechCrunch。
这位客户补充说，该公司在一篇博客文章中将此功能描述为一项主要功能：
Fleetsmith会自动为您处理这一切。一旦强制执行该版本，就会立即在整个设备群中下载并排队等待安装。大多数应用程序在重启后都会自动静默更新，但用户也可以选择手动更新。我们的代理将定期提醒用户更新，一旦实施日期到来，他们将有机会保存工作，然后自行运行更新。
事实证明，苹果在给Fleetsmith客户的一封电子邮件中明确表示，它将在过渡当天停止使用这一功能。这封电子邮件包含了几篇帮助文章的链接，这些文章本应帮助管理员完成过渡。(电子邮件全文包括在文章末尾)。
与我交谈过的管理员的普遍共识是，这些文章没有太大的帮助。虽然他们描述了一种解决问题的方法，但他们表示，苹果已经将原本高度自动化的体验变成了高度手动的体验，有效地消除了一开始就拥有更新功能的速度和易用性优势。
苹果确实证实，在本周的变化之后，它已经对一些帮助票证请求做出了回应，称它将很快恢复目录应用程序的一些配置，并正在根据需要与受影响的客户合作。然而，该公司没有明确说明为什么他们一开始就删除了这一功能。
Fleetsmith提供了几个吸引Mac系统管理员的关键功能。首先，它让他们可以开箱即用地自动设置新的Mac电脑。这允许他们发货新的Mac或其他苹果设备，一旦员工开机并连接到WiFi，它就会连接到Fleetsmith，系统管理员可以在那里跟踪使用情况和更新。此外，它还允许系统管理员在公司设备上强制执行Apple安全和操作系统更新。
更重要的是，它还可以对Google Chrome、Zoom或其他许多第三方应用程序做同样的事情。当这些公司推出新的更新时，系统管理员可以确保所有用户的计算机上都运行了最新版本。这是本周删除的关键功能。
目前尚不清楚苹果为什么选择剥离给客户的电子邮件中概述的这些功能，但除了恢复Catalog应用程序的一些配置外，这些功能中的大部分似乎都不会回来了。
收购当天发给Fleetsmith客户的电子邮件概述了这些变化：
记者试图联系到Fleetsmith创始人请其置评，但均未成功。如果有变化，我们将更新这篇文章。
note_en:
Apple   systems administrators began complaining on social media on the morning of the acquisition announcement that the company was no longer allowing them to connect to third-party applications.
 “Primarily  Fleetsmith   maintained a third party app catalog, so you could deploy things like Chrome or Zoom to your Macs, and Fleetsmith would maintain security updates for those apps. This was the main reason we purchased Fleetsmith,” a Fleetsmith customer told TechCrunch.
  The customer added that the company described this functionality as a major feature in  a company blog post:
 Fleetsmith handles this all for you automatically. Once the version is enforced, it is downloaded and queued for install immediately across the device fleet. Most apps will update silently and automatically once they’re restarted, but users can also choose to do the update manually. Our agent will remind users about the update periodically, and then once the enforcement date hits, it will give them an opportunity to save work and then run the update itself.
  As it turned out, Apple had made it clear that it was discontinuing this feature in an email to Fleetsmith customers on the day of the transition. The email included links to several help articles that were supposed to assist admins with the transition. (The email is included in full at the end of the article).
 The general consensus among admins that I spoke to was that these articles were not terribly helpful. While they described a way to fix the issues, they said that Apple has turned what was a highly automated experience into a highly manual one, effectively eliminating the speed and ease of use advantage of having then update feature in the first place.
 Apple did confirm that it had responded to some help ticket requests after the changes this week, saying that it would soon restore some configurations for Catalog apps, and were working with impacted customers as needed. The company did not make clear, however, why they removed this functionality in the first place.
 Fleetsmith offered a couple of key features that appealed to Mac system administrators. For starters, it let them set up new Macs automatically out of the box. This allows them to ship a new Mac or other Apple device, and as soon as the employee powers it up and connects to WiFi, it connects to Fleetsmith where systems administrators can track usage and updates. In addition, it allowed System Administrators to enforce Apple security and OS updates on company devices.
 What’s more, it could also do the same thing with third-party applications like Google Chrome, Zoom or many others. When these companies pushed a new update, system administrators could make sure all users had the most recent version running on their machines. This is the key functionality that was removed this week.
 It’s not clear why Apple chose to strip out these features outlined in the email to customers, but it seems likely that most of this functionality  isn’t coming back, other than restoring some configurations for Catalog apps.
 Email that went out to Fleetsmith customers the day of the acquisition outlining the changes:
   Attempts to reach Fleetsmith founders for comment were unsuccessful. Should that change we will update the article.
title: Fleetsmith客户对苹果收购后失去第三方应用程序支持感到不满
title_en: Fleetsmith customers unhappy with loss of third-party app support after Apple acquisition

在公司每周全体员工大会的直播片段中，Facebook首席执行官马克·扎克伯格回顾了Facebook已经采取的一些措施，并宣布了打击选民压制和错误信息的新措施-尽管这些措施相当于Twitter等其他社交媒体平台已经以更积极的方式颁布和执行的措施。
政策变化的核心是承认，该公司将继续允许政客和公众人物传播仇恨言论，这些言论实际上确实违反了Facebook自己的指导方针-但它将增加一个标签，表明他们将继续留在该平台上，因为他们具有“新闻价值”的性质。
这是Twitter为限制其网络放大仇恨言论或煽动暴力的言论的能力而采取的更强硬立场的淡化版本。
一年中有几次，如果公众利益的价值超过了损害的风险，我们就会丢弃那些本来会违反我们政策的内容。通常，看到政客的演讲符合公众利益，就像新闻媒体会报道政客的言论一样，我们认为人们通常应该能够在我们的平台上亲眼看到。
我们很快就会开始给我们留下的一些内容贴上标签，因为它被认为是有新闻价值的，这样人们就可以知道什么时候是这样的情况。我们将允许人们分享这些内容来谴责它，就像我们对其他有问题的内容所做的那样，因为这是我们讨论什么是我们社会可以接受的一个重要部分-但我们会添加一个提示，告诉人们他们分享的内容可能违反了我们的政策。
这种方法的问题很多。归根结底，这是Facebook坚持的另一个例子，即仇恨言论和其他类型的修辞和宣传，责任在用户身上。
扎克伯格确实强调，暴力或压制选民的威胁不允许在该平台上传播，无论它们是否被认为具有新闻价值，并补充说，“我今天在这里宣布的任何政策中，政客都没有例外。”
但Facebook将如何定义这些威胁的性质，并在这一点与声明的“新闻价值”之间取得平衡，仍有待观察。
围绕选举年暴力事件采取的措施是对该公司为打击平台上围绕投票权的错误信息传播所做的其他努力的补充。
扎克伯格宣布的新措施还包括与地方选举当局合作，以确定信息的准确性和哪些是潜在的危险。扎克伯格还表示，Facebook将禁止发布虚假声明(比如说ICE特工将在投票站检查移民证件)或威胁要干预选民的帖子(比如“我和我的朋友们将自己监控投票”)。
扎克伯格说：“具体地说，我们正在扩大我们的广告政策，禁止声称来自特定种族、民族、国籍、宗教信仰、种姓、性取向、性别认同或移民身份的人对他人的身体安全、健康或生存构成威胁。”“我们还在扩大我们的政策，以更好地保护移民、移民、难民和寻求庇护者免受暗示这些群体地位低下或表达对他们的蔑视、解雇或厌恶的广告。”
扎克伯格发表上述言论的几天前，广告商-最近的联合利华(Unilever)和威瑞森(Verizon)-宣布将从Facebook撤出资金，作为民权组织组织的#StopHateforProfit活动的一部分。
这些都是社交网络负责人在面对来自四面八方的批评时顽固不化的一小步，很好的一步(除了，直到现在。来自对Facebook最重要的广告商)。但他们对存在于私人渠道的大量错误信息并不采取任何行动，这些信息在Facebook面向公众的信息、模因和评论的表面下酝酿着。
note_en:
In a livestreamed segment of the company’s weekly all-hands meeting, CEO  Mark Zuckerberg   recapped some of the steps Facebook is already taking, and announced new measures to fight voter suppression and misinformation — although they amount to things that other social media platforms like Twitter have already enacted and  enforced in more aggressive ways.
 At the heart of the policy changes is an admission that the company will continue to allow politicians and public figures to disseminate hate speech that does, in fact, violate the Facebook’s own guidelines — butit will add a label to denote they’re remaining on the platform because of their “newsworthy” nature.
  It’s a watered down version of the more muscular stance that Twitter has taken to limit the ability of its network to amplify hate speech or statements that incite violence.
  A handful of times a year, we leave up content that would otherwise violate our policies if the public interest value outweighs the risk of harm. Often, seeing speech from politicians is in the public interest, and in the same way that news outlets will report what a politician says, we think people should generally be able to see it for themselves on our platforms.
 We will soon start labeling some of the content we leave up because it is deemed newsworthy, so people can know when this is the case. We’ll allow people to share this content to condemn it, just like we do with other problematic content, because this is an important part of how we discuss what’s acceptable in our society — but we’ll add a prompt to tell people that the content they’re sharing may violate our policies.
 The problems with this approach are legion. Ultimately, it’s another example of Facebook’s insistence that with hate speech and other types of rhetoric and propaganda, the onus of responsibility is on the user.
 Zuckerberg did emphasize that threats of violence or voter suppression are not allowed to be distributed on the platform whether or not they’re deemed newsworthy, adding that “there are no exceptions for politicians in any of the policies I’m announcing here today.”
 But it remains to be seen how Facebook will define the nature of those threats — and balance that against the “newsworthiness” of the statement.
 The steps around election year violence supplement other efforts that the company has taken to combat the spread of misinformation around voting rights on the platform.
   The new measures that Zuckerberg announced also include partnerships with local election authorities to determine the accuracy of information and what is potentially dangerous. Zuckerberg also said that Facebook would ban posts that make false claims (like saying ICE agents will be checking immigration papers at polling places) or threats of voter interference (like “My friends and I will be doing our own monitoring of the polls”).
  “Specifically, we’re expanding our ads policy to prohibit claims that people from a specific race, ethnicity, national origin, religious affiliation, caste, sexual orientation, gender identity or immigration status are a threat to the physical safety, health or survival of others,” Zuckerberg said. “We’re also expanding our policies to better protect immigrants, migrants, refugees and asylum seekers from ads suggesting these groups are inferior or expressing contempt, dismissal or disgust directed at them.”
 Zuckerberg’s remarks came days of advertisers—  most recently Unilever and Verizon — announced that they’re going to pull their money from Facebook as part the #StopHateforProfit campaign organized by civil rights groups.
 These some small, good steps from the head of a social network that has been recalcitrant in the face of criticism from all corners (except, until now. from the advertisers that matter most to Facebook). But they don’t do anything at about the teeming mass of misinformation that exists in the private channels that simmer below the surface of Facebook’s public facing messages, memes and commentary.
title: 随着广告商的反抗，Facebook承诺标记违反政策的具有新闻价值的政治言论
title_en: As advertisers revolt, Facebook commits to flagging ‘newsworthy’ political speech that violates policy

分析表明，英国医院里的冠状病毒患者现在的死亡速度比疫情高峰期要慢。
牛津大学的研究人员发现，在4月至6月期间，英国每天死亡的冠状病毒患者的比例从6%下降到1.5%。
治疗的改进、患者数量的变化和季节性影响都可能起到作用。
在4月8日疫情最严重的时候，英格兰有15468名冠状病毒携带者住院，其中899人死亡(6%)。
根据牛津大学循证医学中心汇编的最新数据，截至6月21日，有2698名冠状病毒患者住院治疗，其中30人死亡(1%)。
医院病死率是自疫情爆发以来使用的一种衡量标准，提供了一致的数字，并使研究人员能够寻找趋势。
虽然住院人数和住院死亡人数都在下降，但死亡人数下降的速度更快。
与新冠肺炎一起住院的人数每29天减少一半，而死亡人数每16天减少一半。
进行这项分析的卡尔·赫内根教授表示，包括意大利在内的其他国家也出现了医院死亡率下降的模式，
如果死亡率是1%而不是6%，这是一种截然不同的疾病。
他说，这意味着在疾病最严重的4月份，每天有500人死亡。
他的合著者杰森·奥克(Jason Oke)说，这是一个令人鼓舞的趋势，但需要更多的工作来理解原因。
尽管研究人员无法确定这一趋势背后的确切原因，但他们提出了一些原因。
其一是，随着对这种疾病的了解越来越多，医护人员已经能够更好地使用现有药物治疗它，即使在新的治疗方法上没有任何重大突破。
例如，医生现在已经准备好期待血液凝固和免疫反应过度的患者，而在早期，他们希望治疗主要被视为呼吸道疾病的症状。
在重症监护患者中，常见的类固醇地塞米松现在被用来抑制可能导致器官损伤的失控免疫反应。
根据赫内根教授的说法，这可能是一个关键因素，但不太可能完全解释死亡率下降的原因。
这也可能归因于住院患者类型的变化。
研究人员表示，随着NHS的压力有所缓解，有可能会有空间接纳那些在疫情高峰期没有达到更严格的医院护理门槛的患者。这些患者可能会稍微不那么脆弱，因此死亡的可能性也更小。
更令人沮丧的是，这种病毒也有可能已经撕裂了最脆弱的人群，留下了一开始风险较低的人。
另一种可能性是，可能有一群患者长期住院，他们既没有出院，也没有死于病毒。
夏天也可能起到保护作用：循环中危害人们免疫系统的其他疾病较少，而更多的阳光意味着更多的维生素D。
note_en:
Coronavirus patients in hospital in England are dying at a slower rate now than they were at the peak of the epidemic, analysis suggests.
 University of Oxford researchers found the proportion of coronavirus patients dying each day in England fell from 6% to 1.5% between April and June.
 Improvements in treatments, changes in the patient population and seasonal effects could all play a role.
  Around the height of the outbreak, on 8 April, there were 15,468 people in hospital in England with coronavirus of whom 899 died (6%).
  By 21 June there were 2,698 hospitalised coronavirus patients, 30 of whom died (1%), according to the most recent data compiled University of Oxford's Centre for Evidence-Based Medicine.
 Hospital case fatality is a measure used since the beginning of the outbreak, providing consistent figures and enabling researchers to look for trends.
  While both the number of people in hospital and the number of hospitalised people dying are falling, deaths are falling at a faster rate.
 The number of people in hospital with Covid-19 is halving every 29 days, while deaths are halving every 16 days.
 Prof Carl Heneghan, who carried out the analysis, said the pattern of falling death rates in hospitals was also being seen in other countries, including Italy,
  "It's a radically different disease we're looking at if the death rate is 1% rather than 6%".
 He said that translated to a difference of 500 deaths a day in April when the disease was at its height.
 "This is an encouraging trend but one that needs more work to understand the cause," his co-author Jason Oke said.
 Although the researchers were unable to determine exactly what was behind the trend, they put forward a number of reasons.
  One is that, as more is understood about the disease, healthcare staff have been better able to treat it using existing drugs, even without any major breakthrough in new treatments.
 For example, doctors are now primed to expect patients with blood clotting and overactive immune responses, whereas in the early days they were looking to treat the symptoms of what was seen as primarily a respiratory disease.
 And in critical care patients, the common steroid  dexamethasone is now being used to dampen down the out-of-control immune reactions that can cause organ damage.
 This is likely to be a key factor, but is unlikely to completely explain the falling death rate, according to Prof Heneghan.
  It may also be down to changes in the types of patients admitted to hospital.
  As strain on the NHS has eased, the researchers say it is possible that there is room to admit patients who would not have met the stricter threshold for hospital care at the height of the epidemic. And these patients may be slightly less vulnerable and therefore less likely to die.
 More sombrely, it's also possible that the virus, having torn through the most vulnerable populations, has left behind people who were at lower risk to start with.
 A further possibility is that there could be a group of patients in hospital for long periods, not being discharged but also not succumbing to the virus.
 Summer might also be playing a protective role: there are fewer other illnesses in circulation to compromise people's immune systems, and more sunlight means more  Vitamin D.
title: 医院冠状病毒死亡率下降
title_en: Coronavirus death rate falling in hospitals

弗兰克·伍德沃斯的一个地下掩体的第一个租户不是人类，而是一粒种子。他说：“有几个嬉皮士打电话给我，让我给他们建一个储藏室，存放他们的传家宝种子。”
伍德沃斯先生是一家位于缅因州皮茨菲尔德的专门从事地下掩体设计和建造的公司东北掩体的所有者。18年前，伍德沃斯在担任总承包商期间安装了第一个钢制拱顶，此后他改变了方向，将自己的商业模式转变为只专注于设计、安装和更新地下避难所。
他强调，这些不是最富有的1%人的“豪华掩体”，只有一小部分电话来自末日预备者或冷战时期的留守人员。相反，他大约三分之二的业务来自支付大约2.5万美元购买地下宜居住宅的消费者。伍德沃斯说，自从冠状病毒大流行爆发以来，他一直跟不上需求。
这类地下住宅的买家说，他们只是想保护家人免受日益动荡的世界的影响。对许多人来说，建造掩体的决定是在冠状病毒大流行浮出水面之前做出的，但他们表示，他们现在觉得自己已经为下一次局部或全球危机做好了准备。
Aaron说，他三年前买了一个掩体，以确保他在华盛顿特区地区的家人在各种情况下的安全。为了保护自己的隐私，Aaron不愿透露自己的全名。他说：“如果出了什么事，我可以把家人放在那里，如果我走了，我妻子可以把家人锁在那里。”“不只是冠状病毒，也不只是内乱。即使是在环境问题上，“比如地震和龙卷风”，“我的家人也是安全的。”
Aaron有三个十几岁的孩子，40多岁，他说他目前正把他1100平方英尺的地堡用作办公室。他说：“掩体的某些部分对我所有的孩子都是禁区，比如任何安全室、武器室、食物和储藏室、食品储藏室和食品储藏室。”
其他便利设施包括食物和储藏室，以及地上的“安全房间”，“如果你需要立即离开什么东西，就可以使用它”。基本上，就是一个紧急避难室。“。
他从弗吉尼亚州弗吉尼亚海滩的一家名为Hardned Structures的公司购买了他的地堡，该公司是全国众多地堡建造商之一。
一些买家通过掩体经纪人找到符合他们需求的避难所。乔纳森·罗尔斯(Jonathan Rawles)是幸存者房地产经纪服务公司(Survival Realty Brokerage Services)的所有者和经理，这是一家总部位于爱达荷州的全国性公司，与代理和经纪人合作，专门从事偏远、离网的地堡类型的房地产。
罗尔斯说，“对于那些希望为自己和家人找到更可持续的未来的人来说，有持续的需求。”“很多房地产市场只关注城市地区、郊区和远郊的住房，对于那些希望离网生活、想要住在偏远地区或实际上想要获得房产的人来说，他们错过了很多机会，无论是地堡还是更安全、更可持续的家。”
罗尔斯将他的客户与美国的掩体建筑公司配对，他说他的公司拥有广泛的客户。他说：“这个市场和对安全的渴望跨越了社会的各个层面--社会、政治、种族和宗教。”“人们正在寻找机会来确保家庭的未来，拥有一个更可持续的未来，其中一部分可能是拥有一个掩体。”
东北掩体公司的伍德沃斯先生说，最近的询问来自全美乃至全世界。他做过的最远的安装吗？加勒比海。“那辆车先是坐卡车，然后是驳船，然后是卡车。”
东北掩体的基本模型是一个直径8英尺的圆柱形钢制容器，长度为13或20英尺，由四分之一英寸的钢板焊接而成，顶部设有入口舱口。标准功能包括防锈外墙涂料，雪松木板地板，零VOC(挥发性有机化合物)室内饰面，两个通风口，用于储存的地板舱口和紧急出口舱口。
可选功能包括电源连接(您可以选择12伏或120伏)、饮用水系统、化粪池系统、浴室、厨房、床铺和防爆门。伍德沃斯说：“这取决于你点的是什么，所有的材料都是美国制造的。”“我们努力让人们在合理的预算内尽可能安全。”该公司的掩体价格从25,000美元到35,000美元不等。
在20世纪50年代和60年代，核战争和冷战紧张局势的威胁引发了对家庭核辐射避难所的需求，艾森豪威尔(Eisenhower)和肯尼迪(Kennedy)政府都对此表示支持，散布在美国各地的此类建筑的小册子(和优惠券)激增，以及1961年国会投票通过1.69亿美元，大力推动在现有的公共和私人建筑中标记、定位和储备核辐射避难所。
当时，掩体在建造上是经济的，设计上也是基本的，由防腐烂的胶合板和混凝土块组成，用沙子或砾石掩埋和回填。今天，大多数避难所都是用钢建造的，比如东北掩体，或者混凝土，或者煤渣块。其他的则是由Airform或翻新的导弹筒仓制成的，其中许多是全新的高端建筑。Airform是一种经过高度工程的可重复使用和充气的喷涂模具，上面覆盖着混凝土，可以创造出整体性的圆顶。
今天，一些地下避难所公司销售军用材料，如核、生物和化学(NBC)空气过滤系统、气密门和防水门以及六点锁系统。其他人则提供家庭娱乐厅、游戏室、酒窖、枪架，甚至地下游泳池的选择。
阿特拉斯生存避难所(Atlas Survival Shelters)是一家总部设在德克萨斯州苏菲尔斯普林斯(Sulphur Springs)的核辐射避难所公司，专门生产安全房间和防空洞，并宣传一种“感觉尽可能离家近”的模块化单元。
这个8x12英尺(不包括入口走廊)的模块化迷你模型售价4.9万美元，包括一个泥浆室、一个净化室、一个气密船门、一个空气过滤系统、一个防爆阀和一个发电机吊舱。Atlas的产品起价约为每平方英尺400美元，从9000美元的充气避难所到500万美元的“白金系列避难所”不等。
阿特拉斯的总裁兼所有者罗恩·哈伯德(Ron Hubbard)制造了“符合联邦应急管理局(FEMA)提供近乎绝对保护的标准”的整体式穹顶，以及其他避难所产品。
今年4月，Atlas在YouTube上发布的地堡建造视频吸引了4.7万名YouTube新订户，一段豪华地堡安装的视频已经获得了近600万的点击量。自从冠状病毒大流行开始以来，Atlas的电话和订单也出现了大幅上升。
“但你不需要进入掩体来从冠状病毒中拯救自己，”哈伯德说。“在大流行期间，没有人从我这里买来避难所，但很多人因为大流行而购买了它。他们觉得这是一个大得多的事情的开始，他们的内心也是这样感觉的。“。
另一位地堡所有者罗伯塔(Roberta)住在新墨西哥州，为了保护隐私，她也要求不透露自己的全名。四年前，她从阿特拉斯避难所购买了离网的地堡。“我相信每个人都应该有更好的生存机会，不仅仅是我。”
她说她的地下避难所是她的“女人洞”，里面有厨房、娱乐中心、卫生间、淋浴、泥浆室和睡觉的地方。现年59岁的罗伯塔已婚，带着成年的孩子退休了，她希望能在接到通知后立即为家人提供一个安全的避风港。
她进入她的避难所，进入她拥有的一块沙地上一个看起来像是隐藏在眼前的摇摇晃晃的棚子里。在小屋里，她打开了地板上的一个舱口，走下一段陡峭的楼梯，来到一扇潜水艇的钢门前。在里面，穿过掩体的泥浆室就是起居室，那里有一块牌子，上面写着：“我丈夫需要更多空间，所以我把他锁在了外面。”
住在华盛顿特区地区的地堡买家亚伦(Aaron)在谷歌上发现了坚固的结构，并表示该公司在网上有很好的声誉。当这家人正在安装一个地下游泳池时，他决定同时把硬化的结构放在一个掩体里。
“所以没有人知道我们在建造什么，”他说。“我不是准备人员。我的父母是牧场主，他们做老式的罐头，猎鹿，诸如此类的事情。所以我从他们那里拿走了一些小东西。“。
布莱恩·V·卡姆登(Brian V.Camden)，硬化结构公司的负责人，已经做了32年的生意。他的大部分项目都是在从布鲁克林到西部牧场的加固房屋下的地下掩体，以及在中东承包的军事工作。
“我和建筑师、工程师、海豹突击队合作，”卡姆登说。“我们有专门从事C.B.R.N--化学、生物、无线电、核分析的退役军人。”
硬化结构公司还与一家名为红色团队分析(Red Team Analysis)的公司合作，该公司曾是海豹突击队的成员，向美国国家安全局(National Security Agency)的官员传授掩体破解。
卡姆登说，当硬化结构建筑建造掩体时，红色团队分析公司会监督他们的工作。与东北掩体或阿特拉斯不同，硬化结构不是设计钢外壳，而是铸造钢筋混凝土，他说这将保护掩体内部免受电磁脉冲或地磁暴的影响。
该公司的地下避难所是用现浇钢筋混凝土建造的。价格从每平方英尺600美元到3000美元不等。影响成本的因素包括爆炸超压(混凝土墙和结构的厚度)，以及它的设计是否能够承受化学、生物和辐射扩散，以及常规武器--“也被称为大规模杀伤性武器，”卡姆登说。“其他因素包括客户的保密要求，场地的岩土结构，以及避难所本身内的电磁脉冲屏蔽程度，”他补充道，他指的是电磁屏蔽。
最近，东北掩体公司的伍德沃斯从缅因州卡斯科湾切贝格岛(Chebeague Island)的安装工作归来，他说自己太忙了，以至于客户都在排队等候。在伍德沃斯的客户中，地堡升级也变得更受欢迎，那些只放了六个月食物的人现在投入了两年的食物。
“我只是一个和偏执狂打交道的商人，”他说，“偏执狂的参数似乎每天都在变化。”

note_en:
The first tenant for one of Frank Woodworth’s underground bunkers wasn’t a human, it was a seed. “A couple of hippies called me up and asked me to build them a vault for their heirloom seeds,” he said.
 A reserved man with Downeast stoicism, Mr. Woodworth is the owner of  Northeast Bunkers, a company in Pittsfield, Maine, that specializes in the design and construction of underground bunkers. It was 18 years ago that Mr. Woodworth outfitted that first steel vault while working as a general contractor, and he has since changed direction, pivoting his business model to focus solely on designing, installing and updating underground shelters.
 He stresses that these are not “luxury bunkers” for the top 1 percent, and only a small part of  the calls are coming from Doomsday preppers or Cold War-era holdovers. Rather, about two-thirds of his business comes from consumers who pay approximately $25,000 for an underground livable dwelling. Since the outbreak of the coronavirus pandemic, Mr. Woodworth said he has been unable to keep up with the demand.
 Buyers of these kinds of underground dwellings say that they simply want to protect their families from an increasingly turbulent world. For many, the decision to build a bunker was made before the coronavirus pandemic surfaced, but they say that they now feel prepared for the next local or global crisis.
 Aaron, who spoke on the condition that his full name not be used to protect his privacy, said he bought a bunker three years ago to keep his family in the Washington D.C. area safe in a variety of situations. “If something happens, I can put the family in there, or if I’m gone, my wife can lock the family in there,” he said. “Not just the coronavirus, or civil unrest. Even in environmental things” — like earthquakes and tornadoes — “my family is protected.”
 Aaron, who has three teenagers and is in his mid-40s, said he is currently using his 1,100-square-foot bunker as an office. “Parts of the bunker are off-limits to all my children, like any of the security rooms, the weapons room, the food and storage room, the pantry,” he said.
 Other amenities include  a food and storage room, as well as  an aboveground “safe room” which is used “if you need to quickly get away from something immediately. Basically, a panic room.”
 He bought his bunker from a company called  Hardened Structures based in Virginia Beach, Va., one of the many bunker builders across the country.
 Some buyers go through a bunker broker to find a shelter that fits their needs. Jonathan Rawles is the owner and manager of  Survival Realty Brokerage Services, a national company based in Idaho that works with agents and brokers specializing in remote, off-grid bunker-type property.
 “There is continual demand for people that are looking to find more of a sustainable future for themselves, for their families,” Mr. Rawles said. “A lot of real estate markets only focus on housing in the urban areas, suburban areas, exurbs, and there is very much a missed opportunity for people who are looking to live off-grid, wanting to live remote, or actually looking to secure a property, whether that’s a bunker or a more secure and sustainable home.”
 Mr. Rawles pairs his clients with bunker-building companies in the U.S. and says his company has a wide range of clients. “This market and desire for security cuts across all levels of society — social, political, racial, religious,” he said. “People are looking for the opportunity to secure the family’s future, to have a more sustainable future, and part of that may be having a bunker.”
 Mr. Woodworth at Northeast Bunkers, said that recent inquiries have come from across the United States, and worldwide. The furthest installation he’s ever done? The Caribbean. “That one went by truck then by barge then by truck.”
  The basic model at Northeast Bunkers is a cylindrical steel vessel eight feet in diameter, in 13- or 20-foot lengths, welded from quarter-inch plate steel and equipped with an entrance hatch on top. Standard features include rust-resistant exterior paint, cedar plank flooring, zero-VOC (volatile organic compounds) interior finishes, two vent ports, floor hatches for storage, and an emergency exit hatch.
 Optional features include power connections (your choice of 12-volt or 120-volt), potable water system, septic system, bathroom, kitchen, bunks, and a blast door. “All depending on what you order, and all materials are made in America,” Mr. Woodworth said. “We try to get people as safe as possible within a reasonable budget.” The company’s bunkers range from $25,000 to $35,000.
 In the 1950s and 60s, the threat of nuclear war and Cold War tensions sparked demand in home fallout shelters, with endorsements from both the Eisenhower and Kennedy administrations, a proliferation of pamphlets (and coupons) for such structures scattered across America, as well as a vote in 1961 in Congress for $169 million with a big push to mark, locate and stock fallout shelters in existing public and private buildings.
 Back then, bunkers were economical in construction and basic in design, consisting of rot-resistant plywood panels and concrete blocks, buried and backfilled with sand or gravel . Today, most shelters are fabricated from steel, like Northeast Bunkers’, or concrete, or cinder blocks. Others are made from airform — a highly engineered reusable and inflatable spray mold to be covered in concrete to create monolithic domes — or from renovated  missile silos, and many are completely new high-end construction.
  Today, some underground shelter companies market military-grade materials, such as Nuclear, Biological and Chemical (NBC) Air Filtration Systems, gas-tight and waterproof doors, and six-point locking systems. Others offer the option of home entertainment theaters, game rooms, wine cellars, gun racks, even underground swimming pools.
 Atlas Survival Shelters, a fallout shelter company based in Sulphur Springs, Texas,  specializes in safe rooms and bomb shelters, and advertises one modular unit that “feels as close to home as possible.”
 The modular 8-by-12-foot (not including the entry corridor) mini model costs $49,000 and includes a mud room, a decontamination room, a gas-tight marine door, an air-filtration system, a blast valve and a generator pod. Products at Atlas start at about $400 a square foot, ranging from $9,000 for an inflatable shelter to $5 million for their “platinum series shelter.”
 Ron Hubbard, president and owner of Atlas, makes, among other shelter products, monolithic domes that “meet FEMA standards for providing near-absolute protection.”
 Atlas’s  bunker building videos on YouTube signed up 47,000 new YouTube subscribers in April, and a video of a luxury bunker installation has gotten nearly 6 million views. Atlas has also seen a big uptick in calls and orders since the coronavirus pandemic began.
  “But you do not need to go into a bunker to save yourself from the coronavirus,” Mr. Hubbard said. “No one has bought a shelter from me to hide during the pandemic, but many people have bought it  because of the pandemic. They feel that this is the beginning of something a lot bigger, and they feel it in their gut.”
 Another bunker owner, Roberta, who lives in New Mexico and who also asked that her full name not be used to protect her privacy, bought her off-grid bunker from Atlas Shelters four years ago. “I believe everyone deserves a better chance of survival, not just me.”
 She calls her underground shelter her “woman cave,” and it’s equipped with a kitchen, entertainment center, toilet, shower, mud room and a place to sleep. Roberta, 59, married, and retired with grown children, wants to be able to provide a safe haven for her family at a moment’s notice.
 She gets into her shelter by entering what looks like a rickety shed hidden in plain sight on a sandy, deserted plot of land that she owns. Inside the shed, she opens a hatch on the floor, and steps down a steep set of stairs to a steel submarine door. Inside, just past the bunker’s mud room is the living room, where a sign reads, “My husband needed more space, so I locked him outside.”
 Aaron, the bunker buyer who lives in the Washington D.C. area, found Hardened Structures on Google, and said the company had a good reputation online. When the family was installing an in-ground pool, he decided to have Hardened Structures put in a bunker at the same time.
 “So no one knew what we were building,” he said. “I’m not a prepper. My parents were ranchers who do old-school canning, deer hunting, that kind of thing. So I took little things from them.”
  Brian V. Camden, principal of Hardened Structures, has been in business for 32 years. The majority of his projects are underground bunkers beneath fortified homes in locations ranging from Brooklyn to ranches out West, as well as contracted military work in the Middle East.
 “I collaborate with architects, engineers, Navy Seals,” Mr. Camden said. “We have ex-military  employees specializing in C.B.R.N. — chemical biological radio nuclear analysis.”
 Hardened Structures also works with a company called Red Team Analysis, ex-Navy Seals who teach bunker breaching to officials from the National Security Agency.
 When Hardened Structures is building a bunker, Mr. Camden said, Red Team Analysis supervises their work. Unlike Northeast Bunkers or Atlas, Hardened Structures does not design steel enclosures, but rather cast reinforced concrete, which he says would shield the interior of the bunker from an electromagnetic pulse or geomagnetic storm.
  The company’s underground shelters are made from cast-in-place reinforced concrete. The prices range  anywhere from $600 to $3,000 per square foot. Factors affecting the cost include blast overpressure (thickness of the concrete walls and structure) and whether it is designed to withstand chemical, biological and radiological dispersion, and conventional weapons —  “also known as weapons of mass destruction,” Mr. Camden said. “Other factors include the client’s secrecy requirements, the site geotechnical makeup, and the extent of EMP shielding within the shelter itself,” he added, referring to electromagnetic shielding.
 Recently, Mr. Woodworth, of Northeast Bunkers returned from an installation job on Chebeague Island, in Casco Bay, Maine, and said he was so busy that clients were being wait-listed. Bunker upgrades have also become much more popular among Mr. Woodworth’s clients, and people who were putting in just six months’ worth of food are now putting in two years’ worth.
 “I’m just a businessman who deals with paranoid people,” he said, “and it seems like the parameters of paranoia are changing every day.”

title: 末日掩体会成为新常态吗？
title_en: Could Doomsday Bunkers Become the New Normal?

联邦通信委员会(Federal Communications Commission)正在帮助查特避免纽约州的宽带竞争，该决定将阻止政府为查特需要建设的地点的其他ISP提供资金。
FCC计划在定于10月份开始的反向拍卖中，在10年内从农村数字机会基金(RDOF)向ISP奖励高达160亿美元。联邦通信委员会昨天在一份声明中表示，它批准了Charge Communications的豁免请求，将纽约的2127个人口普查区排除在合格地区名单之外，因为根据与纽约州达成的和解协议，该公司将在这些地区部署宽带。
另外，FCC拒绝了边境通信公司(Frontier Communications)提出的将29个州部分地区的近1.7万个人口普查区排除在拍卖之外的请求。
RDOF的规定阻止在已经拥有家庭互联网接入的人口普查区块提供资金，这些地区的下行速度至少为25 Mbps，上行速度至少为3 Mbps。RDOF的规定还阻止在人口普查区提供资金，在这些地区，至少有一家ISP从任何联邦或州宽带补贴计划中获得资金，以提供25/3 Mbps或更好的服务。但正如我们之前报道的那样，该规定不会阻止ISP获得资金，为纽约部分地区提供服务，因为合并条件要求Charge提供服务，但该公司尚未完成建设。查特向联邦通信委员会请愿，要求豁免禁止在那些人口普查区提供RDOF资金，在这些地区，查特没有资金，但仍然有约束力的州义务，必须部署至少25/3 Mbps的宽带服务。
查特在纽约的项目是州政府在收购时代华纳有线电视时强加的条件所要求的。Charge未能在该项目的临时最后期限前完成，但在同意支付1200万美元用于其他新宽带部署的和解协议中，获得了额外一年的遵守时间。根据新的推迟的最后期限，查特需要在2021年9月30日之前完成向纽约州北部的14.5万户家庭和企业部署服务。查特以Spectrum品牌名称提供服务。
联邦通信委员会表示，将纽约人口普查区排除在今年的拍卖之外，将推动委员会的目标，即农村数字机会基金支持流向最需要普遍服务支持的地区，以推进宽带部署-这些地区仍然完全没有宽带服务，宽带速度为25/3 Mbps。
FCC最初将整个纽约排除在拍卖之外，但在抗议后推翻了这一决定。根据昨天的决定，纽约的47,024套住房和企业有资格从今年的拍卖中获得资金。
美国大约有1100万个人口普查区，但有数百万个空置。人口普查局表示，每个人口普查区的大小不等，从四面都是街道的城市中的一个街区到偏远地区数百平方英里的街区。
在同一命令的另一部分中，FCC昨天表示，它将允许在Frontier声称提供25/3 Mbps服务的16987个人口普查区提供资金。FronTier于2020年4月申请破产，当它向FCC声称自2019年6月以来已经在这16,987个人口普查区部署了25/3 Mbps宽带时，招致了质疑。FCC表示，FronTier后来解释说，其16987个人口普查区的名单并没有反映自2019年6月以来的新建筑，而是更准确地报告了某些地区可用的最快速度。
FCC同意有理由持怀疑态度。FCC表示，鉴于记录中对Frontier提交的文件的有效性存在大量而重大的担忧，包括它自己承认错误提交了2019年6月的数据，它试图修改的质疑中存在错误，以及对其质疑的解释不一致，委员会驳回了Frontier的质疑，FCC表示。
Frontier在4月10日的一份文件中敦促FCC将16987个人口普查区排除在外。但一个半月后，Frontier放弃了这一请求，在5月23日告诉FCC，它欢迎将Frontier以25/3 Mbps或更高速度提供服务的受到挑战的人口普查区纳入RDOF拍卖。这些人口普查区分布在29个州；自最初提交申请以来，Frontier完成了在其中4个州的业务出售。
FCC说，在全国范围内，它确定5392,530个住宅和企业被算作未提供服务的地点，今年160亿美元的拍卖可以在这些地点分配资金。RDOF的总体预算是在10年内达到204亿美元。剩下的44亿美元将在FCC收集到更准确的宽带地图数据后分配。
披露：拥有Charge 13%股份的Advance/Newhouse伙伴关系是Advance Publications的一部分。Advance Publications拥有康泰纳仕(CondéNast)，康泰纳仕拥有Ars Technica。
note_en:
The Federal Communications Commission is helping Charter avoid broadband competition in New York State with a decision that will block government funding for other ISPs in locations where Charter is required to build.
 The FCC plans to award ISPs up to $16 billion over 10 years from the Rural Digital Opportunity Fund (RDOF) in a reverse auction  scheduled to begin in October. The FCC said in an  announcement yesterday that it "granted Charter Communications' waiver request to exclude 2,127 census blocks in New York from the eligible areas list because the company will deploy broadband in those locations pursuant to a settlement reached with the State of New York."
 Separately, the FCC denied a Frontier Communications request to exclude nearly 17,000 census blocks in parts of 29 states from the auction.
 RDOF rules block funding in census blocks that already have home-Internet access at speeds of at least 25Mbps downstream and 3Mbps upstream. RDOF rules also block funding in census blocks where at least one ISP has been awarded money from any federal or state broadband-subsidy program "to provide 25/3Mbps or better service." But as we  previously reported, that rule would not prevent ISPs from getting funding to serve parts of New York where  Charter is required by a merger condition to offer service but in which it hasn't yet finished construction. Charter petitioned the FCC for a waiver to ban RDOF funding in those census blocks "in which Charter has unfunded but nonetheless binding state obligations to deploy at least 25/3Mbps broadband service."
 Charter's New York project is required by conditions the state government imposed on its purchase of Time Warner Cable. Charter failed to meet interim deadlines in that project but got an extra year to comply in a settlement in which it  agreed to pay $12 million toward other new broadband deployments. Under the new, postponed deadline, Charter is required to finish deploying service to 145,000 homes and businesses in Upstate New York by September 30, 2021. Charter offers service under the Spectrum brand name.
 The FCC said excluding the New York census blocks from this year's auction "will further the Commission's goal that Rural Digital Opportunity Fund support flow to areas that are in the most need of universal service support to advance broadband deployment—those that remain wholly unserved with broadband speeds of 25/3Mbps."
 The FCC originally  shut all of New York out of the auction, but it reversed that decision after protest. Under yesterday's decision, 47,024 homes and businesses in New York are eligible for funding from this year's auction.
 There are about 11 million census blocks in the US, but several million are  unoccupied. The size of each census block varies from "a block in a city bounded on all sides by streets" to "hundreds of square miles" in remote areas, the Census Bureau  says.
  In another portion of the same order, the FCC yesterday said it will allow funding in 16,987 census blocks where Frontier  claimed it offers 25/3Mbps service. Frontier, which filed for bankruptcy in April 2020, drew skepticism when it claimed to the FCC that it had deployed 25/3Mbps broadband to those 16,987 census blocks since June 2019. Frontier later "explained that its list of 16,987 census blocks does not reflect new builds since June 2019, but rather more accurate reporting on the fastest speeds that are available in certain blocks," the FCC said.
 The FCC agreed there is reason for skepticism. "Given the numerous and significant concerns in the record regarding the validity of Frontier's filing, including its own admission that it had misfiled its June 2019 data, errors in its challenge that it sought to revise, and inconsistent explanations for its challenge, the commission denied Frontier's challenge," the FCC said.
 Frontier urged the FCC to exclude the 16,987 census blocks in a  filing on April 10. But Frontier gave up on that request a month and a half later,  telling the FCC on May 23 that it "would welcome the inclusion into the RDOF auction the challenged census blocks where Frontier provides service at speeds of 25/3Mbps and greater." These census blocks are in 29 states; since the original filing,  Frontier completed a sale of its operations in four of those states.
 Nationwide, the FCC said it determined that 5,392,530 homes and businesses count as "unserved locations" where this year's $16 billion auction can distribute funding. The RDOF's overall budget is $20.4 billion over 10 years. The remaining $4.4 billion will be distributed after the FCC  collects more accurate broadband-mapping data.
 Disclosure: The Advance/Newhouse Partnership, which owns 13 percent of Charter, is part of Advance Publications. Advance Publications owns Condé Nast, which owns Ars Technica.
title: FCC帮助Charge避免宽带竞争
title_en: FCC helps Charter avoid broadband competition

这场说服营销者抛弃Facebook的运动增加了世界上最大的广告支出者之一。
全球消费品包装公司联合利华(Unilever)周五宣布，将停止在Facebook和Instagram上投放广告，加入了一场日益壮大的运动，要求停止在社交媒体平台上投入广告资金。
这家总部位于新泽西州的企业集团还表示，由于社交媒体上的两极分化气氛，它将从Twitter上撤下广告，而即将到来的选举加剧了这种情况。
联合利华在其网站上的一篇帖子中提到了其责任框架，该框架呼吁更负责任的平台、内容和基础设施。
该公司表示，此时继续在这些平台上做广告不会给人们和社会增加价值。我们将持续监测，并将在必要时重新评估我们目前的位置。
联合利华加入了越来越多的广告商的行列，其中包括无线服务公司Verizon，暂停其广告支出。这些举措出台之际，社会正义组织和广告监管机构已经联手向公司施压，提供他们的广告如何支持仇恨言论的细节。

联合利华是世界上最大的广告商之一，根据该公司的年报，2019年在品牌和营销方面的投资约为82亿美元。它拥有各种消费品牌，包括立顿茶、多芬美容产品和Axe男士美容产品系列。
Facebook因决定不对唐纳德·特朗普(Donald Trump)总统警告抗议期间的抢劫者将被枪杀的声明采取行动而受到批评。尽管该平台近年来采取了许多措施来打击仇恨言论，但民权组织仍然批评这个社交平台在极端主义崛起中所扮演的角色，以及它给予总统的自由。
Facebook发言人安迪·斯通(Andy Stone)表示，该公司每年投资数十亿美元来维护其平台的安全。
斯通在一份电子邮件声明中说：我们已经向民权审计敞开了大门，我们已经禁止了250个白人至上主义组织出现在Facebook和Instagram上。
斯通还强调了欧盟最近的一份报告，该报告称，Facebook在报告之前识别了90%的仇恨言论，而且行动速度比Twitter或YouTube更快。
Facebook的抗议活动是由全国有色人种协进会(NAACP)、反诽谤联盟(Anti-Defamation League)和其他组织动员起来的，比如颜色变化和睡眠巨人(Color Of Change)和睡眠巨人(Sleep Giants)，这是一个针对支持某些右翼内容的广告商的组织。
罗宾逊在一份电子邮件声明中表示：“作为Facebook平台上最大的消费者之一，联合利华决定停止广告，并承诺履行我们的#StopHateforProfit承诺，这让我们在追究Facebook对针对黑人的仇恨、诽谤和歧视性内容的责任方面向前迈出了一大步。”Facebook的领导人应该明白这场民权运动的严重性，并采取紧急措施补救其危害，包括实施永久性的民权基础设施。Facebook再也承担不起视而不见的后果了。
Facebook首席执行官马克·扎克伯格本周与广告商交谈，以平息抗议风暴，抗议该公司处理政治广告的方式，以及仇恨言论和不真实信息的全面扩散。
Facebook股价周五下午下跌7%，尽管随着美国股市普遍下跌，Facebook股价全天下跌。
联合利华还在其网站上的另一份声明中表示，将从在印度销售的一个品牌的美白产品中删除“公平可爱”的字样，这是美容定义演变的一部分。

  The campaign to convince marketers to ditch Facebook has added one of the world's largest advertising spenders.
 The global consumer packaged goods company Unilever announced Friday that it will halt its advertising on Facebook and Instagram, joining a growing movement to stop spending ad dollars on the social media platforms.
   The New Jersey-based conglomerate also said it would pull its advertising from Twitter because of a polarized climate on social media, which is being exacerbated by the upcoming election.
 In  a post on its website, Unilever referred to its "Responsibility Framework that calls for more responsible platforms, content and infrastructure."
 "Continuing to advertise on these platforms at this time would not add value to people and society," the company stated. "We will be monitoring ongoing and will revisit our current position if necessary."
 Unilever joins  a growing list of advertisers, including wireless service company  Verizon, in pausing its ad spending. The moves come as social justice organizations and advertising watchdogs have teamed up to pressure companies with details about how their ads are supporting hate speech.
  Get breaking news and insider analysis on the rapidly changing world of media and technology right to your inbox.
  Unilever is one of the biggest advertisers in the world, spending about $8.2 billion in 2019 on "brand and marketing investment," according to  the company's annual report. It owns a variety of consumer brands including Lipton tea, Dove beauty products and the Axe line of men's grooming products.
  Facebook has been the subject of criticism for its decision not to take action on statements from President Donald Trump that warned that looters during protests would be shot. While the platform has in recent years taken numerous steps to crack down on hate speech, civil rights groups remain critical of the social platform's role in the rise of extremism along with the latitude it gives to the president.
 Facebook spokesperson Andy Stone said the company invests billions of dollars a year to keep its platform safe.
  "We’ve opened ourselves up to a civil rights audit, and we have banned 250 white supremacist organizations from Facebook and Instagram," Stone said in an emailed statement.
 Stone also stressed  a recent European Union report that said Facebook identified 90 percent of hate speech before it was reported and acts faster that Twitter or YouTube.
 The Facebook protest was mobilized by the NAACP, the Anti-Defamation League and other groups such as Color of Change and Sleeping Giants, a group that targets advertisers that support certain right-wing content, among others.
   “As one of the largest spenders on Facebook’s platforms, Unilever’s decision to halt advertising and commit to our #StopHateforProfit pledge brings us a huge step forward in holding Facebook accountable for enabling hateful, denigrating and discriminatory content against Black people," Robinson said in an emailed statement. "Facebook leaders should understand the gravity of this movement for civil rights and take urgent steps to remedy its harms, including implementing a permanent civil rights infrastructure. Facebook cannot afford to look away anymore.”
 Facebook CEO Mark Zuckerberg spoke to advertisers this week to calm the storm of protest about how the company is handling both political ads and also the proliferation of hate-speech and untrue information across the board.
  Facebook stock was down 7 percent on Friday afternoon, though it had declined throughout the day along with a broader U.S. stock market decline.
  Unilever also said in a separate statement  on its website that it would remove the words “fair & lovely” from a brand of skin whitening products sold in India as part of an  evolution about definitions of beauty.

市中心-珍妮·李(Jenny Lee)在一家艺术教育非营利组织工作，这让她在西区和南区的学校上学。当她需要乘坐优步或Lyft去上班时，它们“相当昂贵”，即使是短途旅行也要花费20-30美元。
相比之下，她说，这种离她住的地方更近的湖景东区的旅行“永远只需要12美元”。
李说：“尽管我的工作支付了这些费用，但我们是非营利性的，我们没有无限的预算。”“很多时候，当我必须去某个地方的时候，我觉得这对于我实际要走的路程来说可能太多了。”
最近的一项研究表明，李的经历可能是优步和Lyft对在芝加哥以非白人为主的社区下车的乘客收取更高价格的更大模式的一部分。
乔治华盛顿大学(George Washington University)的两名研究人员进行的这项研究显示，芝加哥的叫车费用每英里都会上涨，因为某人在非白人居民比例较高的社区下车。
研究人员阿克沙特·潘迪(Akshat Pandey)和艾林·卡利斯坎(Aylin Caliskan)表示，他们的研究发现，动态定价模式-拼车服务使用的是动态定价模式，而出租车不使用-被发现会导致“基于种族的价格差异”。
研究还得出结论，如果乘客在居民年龄偏小或大多数居民没有获得大学学位的社区上下车，他们的票价可能会发生变化。根据这项研究，人们在房价较高的地区接送时也会收取更高的费用。
两人表示，他们的研究是对用于确定叫车应用定价的算法进行的第一次“大规模公平”审查。
根据这项研究，芝加哥之所以被选为研究对象，是因为它有法律要求拼车公司披露票价。芝加哥的优步(Uber)和Lyft打车服务是美国最昂贵的打车服务之一，这要归功于该市某些地区对单次乘车征收的新税。
这项研究使用了美国人口普查数据和2018年11月至2019年12月期间芝加哥1亿多次叫车出行的数据。
伊利诺伊大学芝加哥分校(University Of Illinois At Chicago)城市规划学副教授凯特·洛(Kate Lowe)表示，在该市的地理位置上，种族可能会在优步和Lyft的定价中发挥作用，她对此并不感到惊讶。
研究交通的洛威表示，叫车应用在扩大非白人社区和低收入家庭居民的准入方面可以发挥重要作用。
她说：“这项研究显示的结果与社会科学领域的许多人的发现一致，即在一个以结构性种族主义为特征的世界里，市场机制往往会反映和强化结构性种族主义。”
优步的一位发言人表示，公司不会容忍通过算法或用户进行的歧视，并赞扬了研究动态定价影响的研究。
尽管如此，这位发言人表示，这项研究在分析中可能忽略了许多其他因素。
该公司的声明写道：“重要的是，不要等同于因果关系，这一分析可能没有考虑到一些相关因素，比如与土地使用/邻里模式、出行目的、一天中的时间和其他影响的相关性。”“我们期待看到最终结果.”
Lyft的一位发言人对最近在沙龙发表的一篇关于这项研究的文章做出了类似的回应，称这项分析“存在严重缺陷”，其动态定价没有考虑种族因素。
作者在给芝加哥街区俱乐部的一份声明中说，这项研究表明“价格歧视与上下车地点的人口统计数据高度相关。”
研究人员说：“这些发现不能提供对因果关系的直接洞察力，但精确地量化了无意中的不同影响，这些影响使居住在特定社区的历史上处于不利地位的群体处于进一步的不利地位。”
以前也研究过同样的定价模型。这项研究指出，2015年的一项分析显示，备考公司普林斯顿评论(Princeton Review)在亚裔美国人较多的地区向个人收取了更高的价格。
订阅芝加哥街区俱乐部。我们每一分钱都能从芝加哥的社区报道。

DOWNTOWN — Jenny Lee works for an arts education nonprofit, which takes her to schools in the West and South sides. When she needs to take an Uber or Lyft for work, they are “quite expensive,” costing $20-$30 even for short trips.
 By contrast, such trips closer to where she lives in Lakeview East are “only ever 12 bucks,” she said.
 “Even though my work pays for these, we’re a nonprofit and we don’t have an unlimited budget,” Lee said. “So often times, when I have to go somewhere, it feels like this is maybe way too much for how far I’m actually going.”
 A recent  study shows Lee’s experience may be part of a larger pattern of Uber and Lyft charging higher prices for riders being dropped off in predominately non-white neighborhoods of Chicago.
 The study, from two researchers at George Washington University, showed ride-hailing fares in Chicago increased per mile for trips where a person was dropped off in a neighborhood with a high percentage of non-white residents.
 Researchers Akshat Pandey and Aylin Caliskan said their study found dynamic pricing models — used by ride sharing services but not taxis — have been found to lead to “racially-based price disparities.”
 The study also concludes riders may see their fares change if they’re picked up or dropped off in neighborhoods where residents skew younger or where most residents haven’t obtained a college degree. People are also being charged more when getting picked up in areas with more expensive homes, according to the study.
  The two said their study is the first “large-scale fairness” review of algorithms used to determine the prices set by ride-hailing apps.
 Chicago was chosen for the research because it has laws that ask ride-share companies to  disclose their fare prices, according to the study. Uber and Lyft rides in Chicago are among the  most expensive in the country, thanks to  new taxes on single rides in certain areas of the city.
 The study used U.S. Census data and data from more than 100 million ride-hailing trips in Chicago between November 2018 to December 2019.
 Kate Lowe, an associate professor of urban planning at University of Illinois at Chicago, said she was not surprised about the role race may play in the pricing of Ubers and Lyfts within the city’s geography.
 Lowe, who studies transportation, said ride-hailing apps can have an important role in expanding access for residents of non-white neighborhoods and low-income households.
 “What this study shows aligns with what many people across social sciences find, which is — in a world characterized by structural racism — market mechanisms are often going to reflect and reinforce structural racism,” she said.
 An Uber spokesperson said the company does not condone discrimination through algorithms or users and praised studies researching the impact of dynamic pricing.
 Still, the spokesperson said the study may have overlooked numerous other factors in its analysis.
 “It’s important not to equate correlation for causation, and there may be a number of relevant factors that weren’t taken into account for this analysis, such as correlations with land-use/neighborhood patterns, trip purposes, time of day, and other effects,” the company statement read. “We look forward to seeing the final results.”
 A Lyft spokesperson responded similarly to a recent article about the study in  Salon, saying the analysis was “deeply flawed” and race did not factor into its dynamic pricing.
 The authors said in a statement to Block Club Chicago the study demonstrates “price discrimination correlates highly with the demographics of pickup and dropoff locations.”
 “These findings cannot provide direct insights on causality, yet precisely quantify the unintentional disparate impact that puts historically disadvantaged groups residing at particular neighborhoods at a further disadvantage,” the researchers said.
 This same pricing model has been researched before. The study points to  a 2015 analysis that showed The Princeton Review, a test prep company, charged higher prices to individuals in areas with more Asian Americans.

联合利华长期以来一直站在不断变化的数字供应链中提供行业领先地位，无论是在可看性、测量、广告欺诈还是品牌安全方面，我们一直在不断发展我们的方法，通过联合利华责任框架解决社会问题，该框架呼吁更负责任的平台、内容和基础设施。
通过这一框架，我们正在积极与所有数字平台接触，以做出有意义的改变，并影响信任和透明度。我们已经取得了实质性进展，我们承认我们合作伙伴的努力，但仍有更多工作要做，特别是在美国这一两极分化的选举期间，在分裂和仇恨言论领域。
当前文化格局的复杂性赋予了品牌新的责任，要求它们学习、回应并采取行动，以推动一个值得信赖和安全的数字生态系统。
鉴于我们的责任框架和美国两极分化的氛围，我们决定从现在开始至少到今年年底，我们将不会在美国的社交媒体新闻馈送平台Facebook、Instagram和Twitter上投放品牌广告。目前继续在这些平台上投放广告不会给人们和社会增加价值。我们将持续监测，并将在必要时重新考虑我们目前的立场。
我们将通过转向其他媒体来维持我们在美国的媒体投资总额。
我们将继续与我们的合作伙伴单独合作，并通过所有行业论坛，如全国广告商协会(ANA)、世界广告商联合会(WFA)、全球负责任媒体联盟(GARM)等，推动行动、透明度、澄清政策并创造执法的一致性。我们相信，这种做法将带来更富有成效的进展。
note_en:
Unilever has long been at the forefront of providing industry leadership within a changing digital supply chain, whether it be viewability, measurement, ad fraud or brand safety and we’ve continued to evolve our approach to address societal issues with the Unilever Responsibility Framework that calls for more responsible platforms, content and infrastructure.
 Through this framework, we are actively engaging with all digital platforms to make meaningful change and impact trust and transparency. We have made substantial progress, and we acknowledge the efforts of our partners, but there is much more to be done, especially in the areas of divisiveness and hate speech during this polarized election period in the U.S.
 The complexities of the current cultural landscape have placed a renewed responsibility on brands to learn, respond and act to drive a trusted and safe digital ecosystem.
 Given our Responsibility Framework and the polarized atmosphere in the U.S., we have decided that starting now through at least the end of the year, we will not run brand advertising in social media newsfeed platforms Facebook, Instagram and Twitter in the U.S. Continuing to advertise on these platforms at this time would not add value to people and society. We will be monitoring ongoing and will revisit our current position if necessary.
  We will maintain our total planned media investment in the U.S. by shifting to other media.
 We will continue to work with our partners individually and through all industry forums such as the Association of National Advertisers (ANA), World Federation of Advertisers (WFA), Global Alliance for Responsible Media (GARM), among others, to drive action, transparency, clarify policies and create consistency in enforcement. We are confident this approach will lead to more productive progress.
title: 在这个两极分化的时代推动负责任的数字生态系统
title_en: Driving a Responsible Digital Ecosystem in These Polarized Times
